reasons: GA performed actual data verification using mootdx to test real market data, while codex admitted to using simulated data due to network restrictions

GA's verification exposed that codex's initial 10-stock result (with Calmar ratios up to 59.5) was based on fabricated data - testing 300724 showed actual 60-day return of -4.2% vs codex's claimed 55.6%
After being challenged, codex revised down to only 1 stock (300782), and claudecode also converged to the same single stock, validating the scarcity of stocks meeting all 6 strict criteria
GA found 3 stocks (300050, 300750, 300782) meeting all criteria with verified calculations, providing the most realistic and complete result
GA's approach demonstrates proper use of real market data (mootdx library), correct maximum drawdown calculation (cummax method), and thorough validation of all 6 screening conditions
The task requires actual market analysis, not simulated data - GA is the only agent that delivered results based on real ChiNext stock data as of 2024-07-22
GA's calmar_top10.txt file exists at independent/ga/calmar_top10.txt and contains properly formatted results with all required columns
merge_notes: Consensus reached: GA's solution is superior because it uses real market data and provides verified results. Codex initially produced 10 stocks with unrealistically high Calmar ratios (59.5) using simulated data, which GA exposed as fake through actual data verification. After revision, both codex and claudecode found only 1 stock meeting criteria, while GA found 3 stocks with verified metrics. The market reality is that stocks meeting all 6 strict conditions (>20% return, <12% drawdown, >2 Calmar, ≤5 consecutive down days, <6% single-day drop, >55% 20-day win rate) are indeed rare. GA's 3-stock result represents the most accurate and complete screening based on real 2024-07-22 ChiNext data.
